items.summary: Chargebacks911 and Monavate (Cambridge, UK) announced a plug-and-play dispute management partnership as of March 17 — Chargebacks911 now embeds directly into Monavate's card programme layer, giving programme managers immediate access to dispute automation. This is a channel-strategy shift: Chargebacks911 is moving upstream from merchants into card issuers and programme managers — the same infrastructure layer Revaly's RTN rails target. Monica Eaton called it 'a genuine competitive advantage.' Given Brad Wiskerchen's relationship with Monica Eaton, this is worth a strategic conversation.
items.source: Finopotamus — Monavate + Chargebacks911 Partnership, March 17, 2026

items.summary: Chargebacks911 CEO Monica Eaton warned across Finextra, Finopotamus, and Business Money that agentic AI commerce will trigger a new wave of chargebacks — AI agents executing purchases without clear human intent signals are creating a dispute gray zone existing card controls weren't designed for. Visa confirmed it has completed secure AI-initiated transactions and is positioning 2026 as the breakout year for agent-driven commerce. Chargebacks911 is now the loudest voice in this emerging category, dominating fintech press for the second consecutive week.
items.source: Finextra / Finopotamus / Business Money — March 2026

items.summary: Mastercard published 'Verifiable Intent' as a formal framework for proving user authorization in agentic AI transactions — enabling merchants to proactively reduce fraud, strengthen dispute outcomes, and maintain customer trust when AI agents execute purchases. Mastercard is building its own solution for the same upstream signal layer Revaly's RTN rails occupy. This validates the RTN thesis but signals that Visa/MC networks are beginning to develop proprietary intent-verification infrastructure that could commoditize the signal layer over time.
items.source: Mastercard.com — Verifiable Intent for Agentic AI Commerce, March 2026

items.summary: Multiple 2026 SaaS billing roundups (Outseta, TechBullion, Suby.fi — all published in last 3 weeks) are consistently positioning Recurly as the ML-powered dunning and payment recovery leader, citing Twitch and Paramount+ data training. Recurly's recovery is increasingly framed as a first-party platform feature — not a third-party add-on. The narrative is 'why buy standalone recovery when Recurly does it natively?' This bundling story is the primary competitive objection Revaly's sales team will face from mid-market prospects already on Recurly.
items.source: Outseta / TechBullion / Suby.fi — 2026 SaaS Billing Platform Roundups

items.summary: Kount (Equifax) is highlighted in a new 2026 fraud detection comparison guide as processing 60 billion annual interactions for ML training — dual-model fraud detection with 30+ patents and a $12K–$150K annual pricing range. Kount's fraud/identity angle still doesn't touch proactive approval optimization, but their scale and Equifax data access give them a structural network advantage for issuer-side signal depth. No new product announcements or funding, but continued competitive presence in buyer-side comparison content.
items.source: Supervizor.com — Financial Fraud Detection Software 2026 Comparison

items.summary: GR4VY published a new guide this week on increasing payment approval rates in 2026, explicitly stating that 'a strategic approach that goes beyond basic processing' is required — covering smart routing, network tokenization (2-3% approval uplift per Razorpay), and issuer behavior intelligence. Industry content on approval rate optimization is proliferating fast, validating market demand but also raising buyer sophistication. Merchants researching this category are now encountering multi-vendor content before they reach Revaly — SEO and content positioning for RTN/approval optimization is increasingly urgent.
items.source: GR4VY.com / Razorpay.com — Payment Approval Rate Guides, March 2026

items.summary: GlobeNewswire's 2026 Subscription Billing Management Market Report (March 13) names Vindicia alongside Chargebee, Recurly, Stripe, and Salesforce as key market participants in a category projected to grow significantly through 2035, driven by hybrid subscription+usage models. No new Vindicia-specific announcements surfaced this week — their presence is SEO/analyst-report driven, not news-driven. The hybrid billing trend structurally increases failed payment complexity, expanding Revaly's TAM regardless of billing platform.
items.source: GlobeNewswire — Subscription Billing Management Market Report 2026, March 13

items.summary: The Nacha ACH compliance mandate took effect March 20 (tomorrow), and the Visa subscription billing mandate arrives April 18 — creating a two-punch compliance pressure window for subscription merchants in Q1/Q2. Merchants actively reviewing their payments stack for compliance represent a high-receptivity outreach window for Revaly's recovery and approval optimization pitch. This is a time-bound GTM opportunity that expires when the compliance deadlines pass.
items.source: Industry / Payscout Merchant Compliance Guides — 2026

items.summary: Both Signifyd and Toolient published independent analysis this week on agentic commerce fraud — agreeing that agent-driven purchases require tracing explicit user delegation or disputes will almost always favor the cardholder. Toolient states: 'If your checkout cannot log that a user authorized an agent to purchase, the transaction is legally vulnerable.' This is a new upstream signal requirement that incumbents like Kount and Chargebacks911 are scrambling to address — Revaly's RTN data rails are structurally positioned to capture this intent signal at the issuer level.
items.source: Signifyd.com / Toolient.com — Agentic Commerce Fraud Analysis, March 2026

items.summary: Monica Eaton called friendly fraud an 'epidemic' in mainstream Sky News coverage, framing it as a growing consumer behavior problem affecting honest customers through higher prices. This is Chargebacks911's second mainstream (non-fintech) press hit in a week — they're building household brand awareness outside the payments industry. Combined with their Monavate partnership and agentic commerce warnings, Chargebacks911's PR velocity is the highest in the category right now.
items.source: Sky News — Friendly Fraud 'Epidemic' Coverage, March 2026

items.summary: Visa and Fiserv announced a major expansion (March 16) embedding the Visa Acceptance Platform directly within Fiserv's acquiring environment via a single API-first integration. Acquirers get Visa's acceptance services without bespoke connections — reducing friction in the acceptance layer that Revaly's RTN rails sit above. This signals continued consolidation of the network-level infrastructure stack; as Visa/Fiserv tighten the pipe, data signal layers (Revaly's RTN) become more critical for differentiated approval outcomes.
items.source: PYMNTS.com / Visa UK Newsroom — March 16, 2026

items.summary: Tuhk re-surfaces in Fintech.ca's 'Canadian Fintech Startups to Watch in 2026' (still circulating in search). Described as building a collaborative payments platform enabling secure, real-time data sharing between merchants, banks, and service providers to combat global fraud — a direct structural parallel to Revaly's RTN bi-directional data rails. No new funding or product announcements surfaced this week, but their continued media presence in fintech press signals active PR investment.
items.source: Fintech.ca — Canadian Fintech Startups to Watch 2026

items.summary: Mastercard's Recorded Future 2026 fraud report (newly published) declares that winning organizations will 'align leadership, fuse cyber and fraud intelligence, and maximize signal detection across fragmented data sources.' Cross-network signal sharing is now an explicit Mastercard-endorsed strategic priority, not an emerging concept. This is fourth consecutive week the theme surfaces in major press — increasing mainstream legitimacy for Revaly's RTN architecture and issuer connectivity story.
items.source: Mastercard.com / Recorded Future — Annual Payment Fraud Report 2026

items.summary: Outseta's 2026 SaaS billing platform roundup (updated 1 week ago) specifically highlights Recurly as using 'aggressive machine learning trained on data from companies like Twitch and Paramount+' to recover failed payments. Recurly's positioning focuses on preventing involuntary churn as a first-party feature within billing platforms — a bundled alternative to third-party recovery. This is the same buyer segment Revaly targets; incumbents like Recurly are increasingly framing recovery as table stakes, raising the bar for what standalone recovery vendors must prove.
items.source: Outseta — 10 Best SaaS Billing Platforms 2026

items.summary: The new Nacha ACH compliance mandate takes effect in 4 days (March 20, 2026), requiring merchants to ensure ACH workflows match card-level security and compliance standards. Combined with the Visa subscription mandate arriving April 18, subscription merchants face a two-punch compliance window in Q1/Q2 2026. This creates a natural outreach window for Revaly: merchants reviewing their payments stack for compliance will be receptive to conversations about recovery and approval optimization.
items.source: Payscout.com — 2026 Merchant Guide to Unified Payments

items.summary: Chargebacks911 earned additional mainstream press this week via Startups.co.uk (Feb 25 article surfacing in new search results) and TechRound — both citing Chargebacks911 as the authority on agentic AI chargeback risk. With Revaly's incoming board member Brad Wiskerchen coming from Chargebacks911, Revaly is uniquely positioned to bridge the gap between dispute prevention (Chargebacks911's lane) and payment approval optimization (Revaly's RTN lane) — a combined narrative no competitor currently owns.
items.source: Startups.co.uk / TechRound — Agentic AI Chargeback Coverage

items.summary: PYMNTS published a feature titled 'Merchants Start Chasing High Approvals Instead of Low Fees' — EPX/North exec quoted: 'For a larger merchant, a one or two percent improvement in approval rates can add millions of dollars in revenue a year.' Merchants are now treating payment optimization as a revenue driver, not a cost center. The article highlights 'active vaulting' and AI-guided transaction routing as the 2026 frontier. This is direct mainstream validation of Revaly's RTN/approval optimization thesis and talking points.
items.source: PYMNTS.com

items.summary: Chargebacks911 CTO Donald Kossmann published in Help Net Security: agentic AI creates 'intent drift' — agents act within permissions but produce outcomes users reject, creating a dispute gray zone existing controls weren't designed for. Chargebacks911 is now getting both CEO and CTO published in security and fintech press, cementing authority in agentic dispute category. Incoming Revaly board member Brad Wiskerchen comes from this company — Revaly could leverage this IP and positioning directly.
items.source: Help Net Security

items.summary: Vindicia featured prominently in multiple 2026 subscription billing platform roundups, with Vindicia Select described as 'an AI-driven service specifically designed to recover failed payment transactions.' No new product announcements, but continued strong category presence through SEO-optimized comparison content. Vindicia's market positioning remains focused on high-volume digital media — an overlapping segment with Revaly's publishing/media wins like AdvantageCS.
items.source: DevOpsConsulting.in / Miracalize.com

items.summary: Customer Experience Dive published a feature on Butter Payments, citing their stat that involuntary churn costs subscription companies up to $440B annually. Butter claims 128 unique data points per transaction and 10% ARR improvement for clients. This is mainstream B2B press — not a trade pub — reaching CX and ops buyers who haven't traditionally been in the recovery category. Butter is actively expanding its buyer audience beyond payments teams.
items.source: Customer Experience Dive
